item.title: About
item.description: This Site Hello there! Welcome to my brain’s dumping ground. The inspiration to create a personal website came from reading through the monthly discussions on Hacker News about the advantages of doing so. Also, I like reading security blogs and would like to post my own research findings here while practicing my technical writing. That said, I want to use this site, not as a professional portfolio, but more as a means to organize and share my knowledge, ideas, thoughts, and resources.

item.title: Books
item.description: This is my reading list. As you will probably be able to tell, I’m a shill for no starch press. It’s probably the cute art on the covers that got me hooked. Current/Upcoming Reads Practical Malware Analysis - Michael Sikorski and Andrew Honig A Guide to Japanese Grammar - Tae Kim Remembering The Kanji 1 - James W. Heisig Remembering The Kanji 3 - James W. Heisig Neuromancer - William Gibson Count Zero - William Gibson Mona Lisa Overdrive - William Gibson Malware Data Science - Joshua Saxe with Hillary Sanders Backlog Security The Hardware Hacker - Andrew “bunnie” Huang Rootkits and Bootkits - Alex Matrosov, Eugene Rodionov, and Sergey Bratus Practical Binary Analysis - Dennis Andriesse Real-World Bug Hunting - Peter Yaworski Black Hat Python - Justin Seitz Attacking Network Protocols - James Forshaw The Hacker Playbook - Peter Kim Serious Cryptography - Jean-Philippe Aumasson Game Hacking - Nick Cano PoC||GTFO - Manul Laphroaig Novels Snow Crash - Neal Stephenson Pattern Recognition - William Gibson Recommendations Do Androids Dream of Electric Sheep?

item.title: Data Science
item.description: Courses A Clear Roadmap for ML/DL - guide on reddit Andrew Ng’s ML Course - the best?, but need to google for python versions of code deeplearning.ai’s DL Courses - tensorflow eww Books Python Data Science Handbook - Jake VanderPlas Hands-On Machine Learning With Scikit-Learn & TensorFlow - Aurélien Géron Book of R - Tilman M. Davies Data Visualization with JavaScript - Stephen A.

item.title: Goals
item.description: Interests AI / ML Agriculture Drawing Go (board game) Hardware Hiking Language Learning Piano Reading Reverse Engineering Security Space Starcraft Tea Travel Action Items November 2019 finish Practical Malware Analysis - Michael Sikorski and Andrew Honig continue A Guide to Japanese Grammar - Tae Kim continue Remembering The Kanji 1 - James W. Heisig to reach 2000 kanji re-read Neuromancer - William Gibson 40x situps, pushups, squats per day hiking once per week starcraft 1hr after work per weekday build a personal website December 2019 nothing!

item.title: Japanese
item.description: Communities r/learnjapanese Books A Guide to Japanese Grammar - Tae Kim Remembering The Kanji 1 - James W. Heisig Remembering The Kanji 3 - James W. Heisig Grammar Tae Kim’s Grammar Guide - learn you some grammar Kanji koohii - SRS flashcards and shared stories for RTK Reading NHK Easy News - news in easy to understand language; has JLPT labels Listening NHK Radio News Vocabulary Anki Anki Guide Dictionary Jisho Youtube Japanese Ammo with Misa 三本塾 日本語の森 Game Grammar Dogen Tools Rikaichamp Lists awesome-japanese

item.title: Malware
item.description: Books Reverse Engineering for Beginners - Dennis Yurichev Practical Reverse Engineering - Bruce Dang, Alexandre Gazet, and Elias Bachaalany Practical Malware Analysis - Michael Sikorski and Andrew Honig The IDA Pro Book - Chris Eagle Malware Data Science - Joshua Saxe with Hillary Sanders Rootkits and Bootkits - Alex Matrosov, Eugene Rodionov, and Sergey Bratus Practical Binary Analysis - Dennis Andriesse Malware Analyst’s Cookbook - Michael Ligh, Steven Adair, Blake Hartstein, Matthew Richard The Art of Memory Forensics - Michael Hale Ligh, Andrew Case, Jamie Levy, and Aaron Walters Windows Internals - Pavel Yosifovich, Alex Ionescu, Mark Russinovich, and David Solomon Mastering Malware Analysis - Alexey Kleymenov and Amr Thabet Learning Malware Analysis - Monnappa K A Courses hasherezade’s guide - guide on how to start with malware analysis Malware Unicorn’s workshops - Malware Unicorn’s courses and CTF writeups Open Security Training - mostly RE courses Lena’s Reversing For Newbies - RE course Dr.

item.title: Pentesting
item.description: Courses Tulpa’s prep guide for OSCP TJnull’s prep guide for OSCP Books Penetration Testing - Georgia Weidman Hacking: The Art of Exploitation - Jon Erickson Real-World Bug Hunting - Peter Yaworski Black Hat Python - Justin Seitz Attacking Network Protocols - James Forshaw The Hacker Playbook - Peter Kim Metasploit - David Kennedy, Jim O’Gorman, Devon Kearns, and Mati Aharoni Challenges CTFtime - CTF event and score tracker captf list - list of permanent ctfs/wargames Hack The Box - pentesting labs OverTheWire - misc wargames Bug Bounties HackerOne bugcrowd Unsorted https://github.

item.title: Project Ideas
item.description: automate blog IoC collection and countermeasure creation; “blogioc.py” utilize ioc_fanger support loki similar project https://github.com/armbues/ioc_parser https://github.com/stephenbrannon/IOCextractor https://github.com/mandiant/ioc_writer https://github.com/CybOXProject/python-cybox contribute to one of these projects: MITRE CAR eqllib Atomic Red Team Threat Hunter Playbook yara engine running online + personal rules and GUI visualization automate malware sample pulling from online trackers https://github.com/krmaxwell/maltrieve https://code.google.com/archive/p/malware-crawler/ POC EDR GUI with focus on pivoting, visualization, and notetaking port BWAPI to python for use with pytorch go match reviewer with tree history diagram for variations; ability to share notes online go AR app integrated with Leela Zero; see featurecat/lizzie

item.title: Resources
item.description: Goals Books Malware Pentesting Security Blogs Data Science Project Ideas Japanese Tea Starcraft
